data.content:
Президент Кубы
Президент Кубы is the leader who announced the initiation of negotiations with the United States, triggering further public unrest.

Куба
Куба is a country experiencing widespread protests, power outages, and arson attacks on government institutions.<SEP>Куба is a country subject to a U.S. energy blockade since January 2026, which has led to widespread blackouts, and is listed by the U.S. Treasury as prohibited from receiving Russian oil.<SEP>Куба is a country that held secret negotiations with the United States in Havana, facing economic decline and pressure for democratic and economic reforms.

Протесты на Кубе
Протесты на Кубе are widespread demonstrations accompanied by arson and prolonged electricity blackouts.

Ateo Blacked
Ateo Blacked is a source that forwarded the report on the protests in Куба.

Коммунистическая партия Кубы
Коммунистическая партия Кубы is the ruling political party in Куба, with local offices targeted and set on fire by protesters.

Телеграм
Телеграм is the platform where the post containing video and information about the protests was published.<SEP>Телеграм is the platform hosting the post from channel 3299898370, which disseminated Александр Дугин's statement.

США
США is the foreign nation with which Куба's president has announced the start of negotiations.<SEP>США is referenced by Александр Дугин as a geopolitical adversary seeking influence over post-Soviet states.<SEP>США is a country that conducted secret negotiations with Куба, proposing the lifting of trade embargoes and access to Starlink in exchange for political and economic reforms.

Электричество
The protests coincide with a two-week-long total blackout of electricity in Куба.

Енергетична блокада
Енергетична блокада is the U.S.-imposed restriction on oil shipments to Куба, enacted in January 2026, resulting in two total blackouts on the island.

Мінфін США
Мінфін США is the U.S. Department of the Treasury that added Куба to a list of countries prohibited from receiving Russian oil.

Берегова охорона США
Берегова охорона США is the U.S. coast guard that permitted a Russian oil tanker to reach Куба despite the blockade.

Росія
Росія is the source country of the crude oil being shipped to Куба, which the U.S. claims will not benefit financially.

Російський нафтовий танкер
Російський нафтовий танкер is a Russian oil tanker carrying approximately 730,000 barrels of oil destined for Куба, scheduled to arrive on March 31, 2026.

Шрайк Ньюс
Шрайк Ньюс is the source that forwarded the report regarding Дональд Трамп’s statements and the oil shipment to Куба.

Дональд Трамп
Дональд Трамп is the President of the United States who stated that he does not object to the delivery of Russian oil to Cuba, despite the energy blockade, and criticized Cuba's regime as corrupt and unsustainable.<SEP>Дональд Трамп is the former U.S. president who used military force to remove Venezuela's leader Nicolás Maduro and threatened to annex Greenland, prompting reactions from Europe's far-right parties.

NYT
NYT is a news organization that reported the U.S. Coast Guard’s authorization for the Russian oil tanker to reach Куба.

MonoBase
MonoBase is a partner in advertising collaboration with Останній Капіталіст.

Ґрета Тунберг
Ґрета Тунберг is collaborating with Italian far-left allies to launch a campaign against the militarization of Europe.

Останній Капіталіст
Останній Капіталіст is the source of the post, sharing content about the anti-militarization campaign.

Україна
Україна is the recipient of defense industry support from European countries.

Європейські країни
Європейські країни are rebuilding their defense industries to support Ukraine.

Італійські ультраліві союзники
Італійські ультраліві союзники are far-left political allies of Ґрета Тунберг who oppose European rearmament.

Мілітаризація Європи
Мілітаризація Європи is the target of a campaign led by Ґрета Тунберг and her allies, opposing increased defense spending.

ЄС
ЄС (European Union) is the subject of a campaign against militarization and rearmament.

Переозброєння ЄС
Переозброєння ЄС refers to the perceived rearmament of the European Union, which is criticized as lacking public support.

Украина
Ukraine is the country where German soldiers from the Bundeswehr are proposed to be deployed in a peacekeeping capacity.<SEP>Украина is the target of Russia’s military advances, facing expected collapse of its defense and state institutions, but is being supported by Western allies to strengthen its military and resilience.<SEP>Ukraine is the recipient of Western arms shipments, details of which are among the classified information reportedly leaked by АдГ.

Социал-демократы
Социал-демократы is a German political party whose supporters most strongly favor deploying Bundeswehr soldiers in Ukraine.

Левые
Левые is a German political party with the highest number of opponents to deploying Bundeswehr soldiers in Ukraine.

Зеленые
Зеленые is a German political party whose supporters most strongly favor deploying Bundeswehr soldiers in Ukraine.

Опрос
Опрос is a survey indicating public opinion in Germany regarding the deployment of Bundeswehr soldiers in Ukraine, with 44% in favor and 42% opposed.

Бундесвер
Бундесвер is the armed forces of Germany, proposed to be deployed in Ukraine as part of a peacekeeping group.

АдГ
АдГ is a German political party with the highest number of opponents to deploying Bundeswehr soldiers in Ukraine.<SEP>АдГ is a far-right German political party suspected of transmitting classified European Union documents to Russia.<SEP>АдГ is a far-right group in Germany with overlapping membership and ideological ties to Atomwaffen Division, as indicated by arrests during investigations.

Германия
The survey reflects German public opinion on the proposed deployment of Bundeswehr soldiers in Ukraine.

Ультралевые
Ультралевые are far-left populist political groups in Europe that Путин seeks to empower, believing they will be more amenable to Russian interests.

Россия
Россия is a country pursuing a long-term military strategy to achieve its "SVO objectives," expand its buffer zone in Ukraine, and prepare for potential conflict with NATO by 2030.<SEP>Russia is accused of receiving classified information from members of the German far-right party АдГ, including details on defense systems and Western military aid to Ukraine.

data.content:
Белоусов
Белоусов is a Russian official who, alongside Путин, outlines Russia’s military plans for 2026–2030, including the expansion of the SVO goals and preparation for conflict with NATO.

НАТО
НАТО is a military alliance that Russia claims is preparing to attack it by the 2030s, though Russia frames its own actions as defensive.

СВО
СВО (специальная военная операция) is Russia’s stated military campaign aimed at securing "historical lands" in Ukraine, with objectives extending beyond Donbas to include Sumy and Kharkiv.

Ультраправые
Ультраправые are far-right populist political groups in Europe that Путин seeks to empower, believing they will be more amenable to Russian interests.

Путин
Путин is the leader of Russia who openly declares Russia's military intentions, seeks to expand control over Ukrainian territories, and aims to influence European politics by promoting ultraleft and ultraright populists.

2026–2030 Military Plan
The 2026–2030 Military Plan is Russia’s strategic outline for sustained military operations in Ukraine and preparation for confrontation with NATO, presented by Путин and Белоусов.

Исторические земли
Исторические земли is Russia’s ideological concept referring to Ukrainian territories it claims as historically Russian, used to justify territorial expansion.

Буферная зона безопасности
Буферная зона безопасности is a strategic buffer zone Russia aims to establish across Ukraine to enhance its security against NATO and Ukraine.

Грета Тумберг
Грета Тумберг is a public figure who has remained silent on the issue of Iran, where hundreds of her peers are being killed.

Сионизм
Сионизм is referenced as an absent factor in the discourse surrounding Iran, implying its irrelevance to the radical left’s priorities.

Западные леваки
Западные леваки are Western left-wing individuals and groups, including media and bloggers, who have remained silent on the situation in Iran.

Иран
Иран is a country where hundreds of young people are reportedly being killed, and where Western leftists have shown no public response.

Западная культура
Западная культура is the cultural framework targeted for destruction by radical left-wing ideologies, according to the text.

Ультра-левые взгляды и идеи
Ультра-левые взгляды и идеи are radical left-wing ideologies characterized by self-destructive tendencies and support for the erosion of Western culture.

https://t.me/c/3299898370/6
https://t.me/c/3299898370/6 is the URL of the post containing the last paragraph.

2026-01-14
2026-01-14 is the date when the post was published on the Ультра левые и ультра правые channel.

Последний абзац
Последний абзац refers to the final paragraph of the post, which is the content focus.

Ультра левые и ультра правые
Ультра левые и ультра правые is a channel or organization that posted content on 2026-01-14.<SEP>Ультра левые и ультра правые is a channel or platform that disseminates content on extremist ideologies, including white jihadism and neo-Nazi movements.<SEP>Ультра левые и ультра правые is an organization or channel that shares content related to extreme political ideologies.

Sweden
Sweden is enhancing its intelligence capabilities by acquiring radar satellites and related equipment from the Finnish aerospace company ICEYE.

Gazprom
Gazprom is a Russian state-owned energy giant facing financial crisis, forced to sell assets to cover multi-billion debts due to the impact of full-scale war.

RZhD
RZhD is a Russian state-owned railway company experiencing financial decline, selling assets to cover massive debts amid the war’s economic impact.

Resurgam
Resurgam is an organization that publishes analytical articles on geopolitical and economic topics through its website resurgamhub and Telegram channel.

ICEYE
ICEYE is a Finnish aerospace company that signed a contract with the Swedish Armed Forces to supply approximately ten SAR satellites and related equipment.

Greenland
Greenland is a territory sought by Donald Trump for control, viewed by Europe as outside U.S. sphere of influence.<SEP>Greenland is a territory where Trump is alleged to have violated sovereignty, according to Alice Weidel.

Donald Trump has expressed interest in acquiring control over Greenland and has initiated actions against the Russian shadow fleet, linking these moves to U.S. geopolitical strategy.<SEP>Donald Trump is the President of the United States who signed the executive order imposing tariffs on nations supplying oil to Cuba.

Moldova
Moldova is undergoing large-scale modernization of its energy system to reduce dependence on Russian energy infrastructure.

Bella 1
Bella 1 is a tanker belonging to the Russian shadow fleet, detained by the United States in an operation targeting international outlaws.

Viktor Orbán
Viktor Orbán is the Hungarian leader who has been in power since 2010 and now faces a real threat of losing power, affecting both Moscow and Washington.

Russia
Russia is the country whose state-owned enterprises (Gazprom, RZhD) are suffering financial losses due to war, and whose shadow fleet vessels have been targeted by the United States.<SEP>Russia is a country that Alice Weidel asserts must be included in any viable peace agreement for Europe.

Hungary
Hungary is governed by Viktor Orbán and is a geopolitical player whose political stability affects both Russian and American interests.

ResurgamHub
ResurgamHub is the website of Resurgam where analytical articles on geopolitics, energy, and defense are published in multiple languages.

Sophia
Sophia is a tanker belonging to the Russian shadow fleet, detained by the United States alongside Bella 1 in an operation nominally targeting Venezuela.

Russian Shadow Fleet
The Russian shadow fleet consists of vessels used to circumvent sanctions and support international outlaws, including those linked to Venezuela and Russia, targeted by U.S. operations.

Ukraine
Ukraine is the language context for most Resurgam articles, indicating their primary audience and regional focus despite multilingual availability.<SEP>Ukraine is the conflict zone where Alice Weidel condemns ongoing violence and advocates for a negotiated peace settlement.<SEP>Ukraine is represented by the national flag (🇺🇦) in the post, indicating its relevance to the content's context.<SEP>Ukraine is the recipient of potential military and financial support from the West, as discussed in the survey.

Saxony
Saxony is a German federal state where Tino Chrupalla employs the wife of a party colleague in his electoral district.

Baden-Württemberg
Baden-Württemberg is a German federal state where Marcus Frontmayr is a leading candidate for AfD.<SEP>Baden-Württemberg is a German federal state where the antiterrorism center SAT BW is located and where extremist activities linked to Atomwaffen Division and Terrorgram-сцена have been monitored.

data.content:
Saxony-Anhalt
Saxony-Anhalt is a German federal state where an initial nepotism scandal involving AfD occurred, centered on Ulrich Ziegmund’s father’s appointment.

Tino Chrupalla
Tino Chrupalla is a co-leader of AfD who has employed the wife of a party colleague in his electoral district in Saxony for nearly ten years, though he clarified his own wife does not work within AfD structures.

Marcus Frontmayr
Marcus Frontmayr is a leading AfD candidate for Baden-Württemberg, deputy chair of the AfD parliamentary group in the Bundestag, and a close ally of Alice Weidel; his wife works as head of an AfD Bundestag member’s office and previously worked as a journalist for the Russian state newspaper Izvestia.

Ulrich Ziegmund
Ulrich Ziegmund is the main candidate for AfD in Saxony-Anhalt who was accused of securing a high-paying position in the AfD parliamentary environment for his father, with an annual salary exceeding 90,000 euros.

Bundestag
The Bundestag is the federal parliament of Germany where AfD members, including Marcus Frontmayr and Ulrich Ziegmund, hold positions and where nepotism allegations have been reported.

Izvestia
Izvestia is a Russian state newspaper where Marcus Frontmayr’s wife previously worked as a journalist.

t-online
t-online is a media outlet that reported on nepotism cases within AfD at both state and federal levels.

Nepotism Scandal
The Nepotism Scandal involves multiple AfD officials employing relatives in party-related positions, including in parliamentary offices, despite the party’s public stance against such practices.

European Legacy
The European legacy refers to the foundational values and historical achievements of the Federal Republic of Germany within the European context, which Merz seeks to protect.

Alternative für Deutschland
Alternative für Deutschland is a political party characterized as populist by German Chancellor Merz, and is the target of his uncompromising opposition.

German Chancellor Merz
German Chancellor Merz is a political leader who takes a firm and uncompromising stance against the Alternative für Deutschland, emphasizing the protection of Germany's European legacy.

Federal Republic of Germany
The Federal Republic of Germany is the nation whose European legacy German Chancellor Merz is determined to preserve against populist influences.

2022
2022 is the year when the Istanbul Agreement was reportedly prevented by Western states.

German AfD
The German AfD is a political party led by Alice Weidel, advocating for peace negotiations between Ukraine and Russia.

Istanbul Agreement
The Istanbul Agreement is a proposed 2022 peace settlement that Alice Weidel claims was blocked by Western states and would have benefited Ukraine.

Great Britain
Great Britain is a Western state criticized by Alice Weidel for obstructing the 2022 Istanbul agreement.

Zelensky
Zelensky is the leader of Ukraine, whose potential peace outcome Alice Weidel contrasts with current negotiations.

Western States
Western states, led by Great Britain, are accused of blocking the Istanbul Agreement, according to Alice Weidel.

Far-Left and Far-Right Ideologies
Far-left and far-right ideologies are the subject of the post, described as being equally problematic and explained clearly in the content.

ZDFHEUTE
ZDFHEUTE is a source (@zdfheute) that provided original content explaining far-left and far-right ideologies in simple language.

MUCRAINE
MUCRAINE is an Instagram account (@MUCRAINE) associated with Ukrainian symbolism (🇺🇦) and content addressing far-left and far-right ideologies.

YURII_KECHUR
Yurii Kechur is an editor (@yurii_kechur) credited for editing the post.

2026-03-24
The date of the post, 2026-03-24, marks the publication of the report alleging that АдГ transmitted classified EU documents to Russia.

Балтийское море
The Baltic Sea region is an area where Russian sabotage and hybrid activities are monitored, and intelligence on these activities is allegedly leaked by АдГ.

Альтернатива для Германии
Альтернатива для Германии is the German political party, also known as АдГ, suspected of leaking sensitive information to Russian intelligence.

Politico
Politico is the media outlet reporting the allegations against АдГ regarding the transmission of classified EU documents to Russia.

Документы ЕС
Документы ЕС are classified European Union documents suspected of being leaked by АдГ to Russia, including details on defense systems and military aid.

European Commission
The European Commission is the executive branch of the European Union, led by Ursula von der Leyen, responsible for proposing legislation and implementing policies.<SEP>The European Commission is the executive branch of the European Union, led by Ursula von der Leyen, and is subject to dismissal via a successful motion of censure.

data.content:
Patriots for Europe
Patriots for Europe is a far-right political faction in the European Parliament that opposes von der Leyen’s policies on migration, national sovereignty, and cultural progressivism.<SEP>Patriots for Europe (PfE) is an ultraright political group in the European Parliament that initiated two motions of censure in October 2025 and January 2026.

data.content:
Ursula von der Leyen
Ursula von der Leyen is the President of the European Commission and a representative of the center-right European People's Party, serving as a central figure in European political establishment and a target of criticism from both far-right and far-left factions.<SEP>Ursula von der Leyen is the President of the European Commission, facing multiple motions of censure between 2025 and 2026.
data.file_path: D:\ObsidianWiki\GeoSpoiler-RAG\output\normalized\Ультра левые и ультра правые\20.txt<SEP>D:\ObsidianWiki\GeoSpoiler-RAG\output\normalized\Ультра левые и ультра правые\21.txt

data.content:
Far-Left Fractures
Far-Left Fractures refer to political groups in the European Parliament, such as "The Left," that criticize von der Leyen for neoliberalism, militarization, and corporate favoritism.

European Parliament
The European Parliament is the legislative body of the European Union where both far-right and far-left factions initiated votes of no confidence against Ursula von der Leyen in 2025.<SEP>The European Parliament is the legislative body of the European Union where motions of censure are voted upon and where political factions operate.

data.content:
The Left
The Left is a far-left political faction in the European Parliament that criticizes von der Leyen for supporting corporate interests, militarization, and insufficient climate action.<SEP>The Left is an ultraleft political group in the European Parliament that initiated the October 2025 motion of censure.

data.content:
Far-Right Fractures
Far-Right Fractures refer to political groups in the European Parliament, such as "Patriots for Europe," that oppose von der Leyen's policies on migration, sovereignty, and culture.

European People's Party
The European People's Party is a center-right political party in the European Union, of which Ursula von der Leyen is a representative.<SEP>The European People's Party is a centrist political group in the European Parliament that consistently votes against motions of censure against the Commission.
data.file_path: D:\ObsidianWiki\GeoSpoiler-RAG\output\normalized\Ультра левые и ультра правые\20.txt<SEP>D:\ObsidianWiki\GeoSpoiler-RAG\output\normalized\Ультра левые и ультра правые\21.txt

data.content:
Green Deal
The Green Deal is Ursula von der Leyen’s flagship policy aimed at making the EU climate-neutral, criticized by far-right groups for harming industry and by far-left groups for being insufficient and corporate-friendly.

Pfizergate
Pfizergate is a scandal involving non-transparent, multi-billion euro vaccine procurement deals during the COVID-19 pandemic, conducted via private SMS messages between von der Leyen’s administration and Pfizer’s CEO.<SEP>Pfizergate refers to allegations of opacity in COVID-19 vaccine procurement, triggering a motion of censure against the von der Leyen Commission in July 2025.
data.file_path: D:\ObsidianWiki\GeoSpoiler-RAG\output\normalized\Ультра левые и ультра правые\20.txt<SEP>D:\ObsidianWiki\GeoSpoiler-RAG\output\normalized\Ультра левые и ультра правые\21.txt

data.content:
Europes Fortress
Europes Fortress refers to the EU’s migration control strategy involving financial agreements with authoritarian regimes like Tunisia and Egypt to intercept migrants, criticized by the far-left as a human rights violation.

Israel
Israel is a country whose actions in the Gaza conflict are fully supported by Ursula von der Leyen, drawing strong condemnation from far-left factions.

Gaza Conflict
The Gaza Conflict is a military and humanitarian crisis in which Israel’s actions are supported by Ursula von der Leyen, leading to accusations of complicity in violations of international law.

Pfizer
Pfizer is a multinational pharmaceutical corporation involved in the Pfizergate scandal through non-transparent vaccine procurement deals with the European Commission.<SEP>Pfizer is a pharmaceutical company implicated in the Pfizergate scandal over opaque vaccine procurement practices.
data.file_path: D:\ObsidianWiki\GeoSpoiler-RAG\output\normalized\Ультра левые и ультра правые\20.txt<SEP>D:\ObsidianWiki\GeoSpoiler-RAG\output\normalized\Ультра левые и ультра правые\21.txt

data.content:
Tunisia
Tunisia is an authoritarian regime with which the European Union has made financial agreements to intercept migrants, criticized by far-left groups as a human rights violation.

Egypt
Egypt is an authoritarian regime with which the European Union has made financial agreements to intercept migrants, criticized by far-left groups as a human rights violation.

Conflict in Gaza
The conflict in Gaza is cited by ultraleft factions as a reason for criticizing the European Commission’s foreign policy.

Jacques Santer
Jacques Santer was the President of the European Commission who resigned in 1999 following a threat of a motion of censure.

Motion of Censure
A motion of censure is a formal legal instrument in the European Parliament that, if passed, requires the entire European Commission to resign.

Mercosur
Mercosur is a South American trade bloc whose free trade agreements with the EU are opposed by ultraright factions.

Social Democrats
The Social Democrats are a centrist political group in the European Parliament that consistently votes against motions of censure against the Commission.

Liberals
The Liberals are a centrist political group in the European Parliament that consistently votes against motions of censure against the Commission.

European Conservatives and Reformists
The European Conservatives and Reformists (ECR) is a right-wing political group in the European Parliament that initiated the July 2025 motion of censure.

Liga
Liga is an Italian political party that forms the core of the Patriots for Europe faction and initiated the January 2026 motion of censure.

George Piperea
George Piperea is a Romanian MEP from the ECR group who led the July 2025 motion of censure against the von der Leyen Commission.

Brussels
Brussels is the seat of the European Commission and the center of political centralization criticized by ultraright factions.

July 2025 Motion of Censure
The July 2025 motion of censure was initiated by the ECR and ultraright factions over Pfizergate and was rejected on 10 July 2025.

October 2025 Motion of Censure (Social Policy)
The October 2025 motion of censure was initiated by The Left over the Commission’s stance on Gaza and neoliberal economic policy and was rejected on 9 October 2025.

January 2026 Motion of Censure
The January 2026 motion of censure was initiated by Patriots for Europe and Liga over centralization and national sovereignty and was rejected on 22 January 2026.

October 2025 Motion of Censure (Trade Policy)
The October 2025 motion of censure was initiated by Patriots for Europe over EU trade deals with the US and Mercosur and was rejected in early October 2025.

Terrorgram-сцена
Terrorgram-сцена is a decentralized far-right extremist online network, primarily on Telegram, inspired by Atomwaffen Division, characterized by shared ideology, aesthetic, and digital practices mirroring jihadist media and strategies, including "white sharia" and martyr cults.

Stefan Balliet
Stefan Balliet is a far-right extremist who attacked a synagogue in Halle in 2019 and was an admirer of Atomwaffen Division.

SAFE
SAFE is the antiterrorism center of Baden-Württemberg (SAT BW) that published a 2025 report documenting the convergence of neo-Nazi and jihadist extremist ideologies in Germany.

Atomwaffen Division
Atomwaffen Division is a transnational neo-Nazi network promoting white supremacy, blending it with salafist national-socialist ideology, "white jihad," and accelerationist terrorism; it supports Hamas, Al-Qaeda, and ISIS, operates as a decentralized online network, and inspires lone-actor attacks through digital propaganda and gamified violence.

Al-Qaeda
Al-Qaeda is a jihadist organization supported by Atomwaffen Division and referenced within far-right extremist circles as an ideological ally in the context of shared enemies.<SEP>Al-Qaeda is a global jihadist organization that received financial support from Al-Taqwa Bank and had ideological and operational links with Aryan Nations.

data.content:
ISIS
ISIS is a jihadist organization supported by Atomwaffen Division and referenced within far-right extremist circles as an ideological ally in the context of shared enemies.

Hamas
Hamas is a jihadist organization supported by Atomwaffen Division and referenced within far-right extremist circles as an ideological ally in the context of shared enemies.<SEP>Hamas is a Palestinian Islamist organization that published and distributed David Myatt’s works, recognizing him as an authoritative Islamist voice.
data.file_path: D:\ObsidianWiki\GeoSpoiler-RAG\output\normalized\Ультра левые и ультра правые\22.txt<SEP>D:\ObsidianWiki\GeoSpoiler-RAG\output\normalized\Ультра левые и ультра правые\23.txt

data.content:
Halle
Halle is a German city where Stefan Balliet carried out a synagogue attack in 2019, linked to Atomwaffen Division ideology.

Militant Accelerationism
Militant accelerationism is the core ideology of Atomwaffen Division and Terrorgram-сцена, advocating the deliberate destabilization and collapse of democratic societies through violence, terror, and chaos, inspired by Charles Manson’s "Helter Skelter" interpretation.<SEP>Militant Accelerationism is a neo-Nazi magazine that incorporates pop culture and gaming motifs to promote radicalization, including references to "Game of Thrones."
data.file_path: D:\ObsidianWiki\GeoSpoiler-RAG\output\normalized\Ультра левые и ультра правые\22.txt<SEP>D:\ObsidianWiki\GeoSpoiler-RAG\output\normalized\Ультра левые и ультра правые\24.txt

data.content:
White Sharia
White Sharia is an ideological construct adopted by Atomwaffen Division and Terrorgram-сцена, mimicking Islamic sharia law as a framework for white supremacist governance and moral codes.

White Jihad
White Jihad is an ideological concept promoted by Atomwaffen Division and Terrorgram-сцена, adapting jihadist terminology and martyrdom culture to white supremacist extremism.<SEP>White jihad refers to the phenomenon where white supremacist neo-Nazis adopt jihadist rhetoric, symbolism, and tactics, framing their violence as a religiously legitimized struggle.
data.file_path: D:\ObsidianWiki\GeoSpoiler-RAG\output\normalized\Ультра левые и ультра правые\22.txt<SEP>D:\ObsidianWiki\GeoSpoiler-RAG\output\normalized\Ультра левые и ультра правые\23.txt

data.content:
Lone Actor Strategy
Lone Actor Strategy is a tactic adopted by Atomwaffen Division and Terrorgram-сцена, inspired by ISIS, emphasizing decentralized, individual attacks radicalized through online networks without direct command structure.

2025 SAFE Report
The 2025 SAFE Report is a document published by the antiterrorism center of Baden-Württemberg (SAT BW), confirming the convergence of neo-Nazi and jihadist extremist ideologies in Germany.

Saints
Saints refers to the glorification of lone-actor terrorists and shooters within Atomwaffen Division and Terrorgram-сцена, mirroring the jihadist cult of martyrdom.

Pandemic Period
The Pandemic Period refers to the timeframe during which the initial observations on ideological convergence between neo-Nazis and jihadists were made, leading to later documented patterns by 2025.

Белый джихад
Белый джихад (White Jihad) is a hybrid extremist ideology synthesizing white supremacy and radical Islamic discourse, targeting vulnerable individuals for radicalization.

The Turner Diaries
The Turner Diaries is an American racist and neo-Nazi literary text that has inspired radical far-right and white jihadist movements.

Неонацизм
Неонацизм (Neo-Nazism) is an extremist ideology rooted in racial supremacy, which has been adapted and merged with radical Islamic rhetoric in the context of white jihadism.

Игра престолов
Игра престолов (Game of Thrones) is a popular television series whose phrases have been co-opted by neo-Nazi groups to promote white jihadist rhetoric.

Поступь Астреи
Поступь Астреи is a source that forwards content related to extremist ideologies, including white jihadism and neo-Nazi movements.<SEP>Поступь Астреи is the source channel that forwarded the original post discussing the convergence of far-right and jihadist ideologies.
data.file_path: D:\ObsidianWiki\GeoSpoiler-RAG\output\normalized\Ультра левые и ультра правые\24.txt<SEP>D:\ObsidianWiki\GeoSpoiler-RAG\output\normalized\Ультра левые и ультра правые\23.txt

data.content:
Siege
Siege is a British neo-Nazi and accelerationist text that has influenced radical far-right and white jihadist ideologies.

Акселерационизм
Акселерационизм (Accelerationism) is a radical political strategy advocating for societal collapse to hasten ideological revolution, adopted by both neo-Nazi and white jihadist groups.

Инцелы
Инцелы (Incel) refers to a subgroup of men who feel socially marginalized and are often targeted by extremist propaganda promoting misogyny and scapegoating.

Запад
Запад (The West) refers to Western countries where white jihadist movements have emerged through the fusion of neo-Nazi and radical Islamic ideologies.

Подростки
Подростки (Teenagers) are a primary demographic targeted by white jihadist propaganda through gamification, memes, and simplified worldviews.

Социальные низы
Социальные низы (Social underclasses) are individuals from disadvantaged backgrounds who are disproportionately susceptible to hybrid extremist recruitment.

Школьники
Школьники (Schoolchildren) are among the vulnerable populations recruited into white jihadist ideologies through online radicalization.

Synergistic Extremism
Synergistic extremism is a U.S.-based term describing the combined and reinforcing nature of extremist ideologies, particularly between white supremacists and jihadists.

Islamists
Islamists are ideological actors whose terminology, symbolism, and tactics are being adopted by neo-Nazis in the phenomenon of fused extremism.

Ultraleftists
Ultraleftists are political groups referenced in the context of ideological convergence with ultraright movements, though not directly active in the described phenomenon.

Ultrarightists
Ultrarightists, including white supremacists and neo-Nazis, are engaging in ideological and operational fusion with Islamist and jihadist groups.

Cumulative Extremism
Cumulative extremism is a British criminological term used to describe the mutual radicalization between opposing extremist groups, including white supremacists and jihadists.

Fused Extremism
Fused extremism is a term proposed to describe the deep, sustained ideological and operational blending between white supremacists and jihadist movements, characterized by mutual inspiration, symbolic integration, and mutual legitimation.

Composite Violent Extremism
Composite violent extremism is a U.S.-based term used to describe the convergence of different extremist ideologies, including neo-Nazism and jihadism.

Jihadists
Jihadists are militant Islamist groups whose attacks and propaganda are supported and legitimized by neo-Nazi extremists.

Johann von Leers
Johann von Leers was a Nazi storm unit leader who converted to Islam, adopted the name Omar Amin, fled to Argentina, and worked for Gamal Abdel Nasser; he was a radical antisemitic propagandist under Hitler.

François Genoud
François Genoud was a Swiss banker with Nazi sympathies who financed the protection of Adolf Eichmann and supported Islamist groups; he was a key financial backer of extremist networks.

Omar Amin
Omar Amin is the Islamic name adopted by Johann von Leers after his conversion to Islam; he remained an antisemitic ideologue and operated in postwar Arab political circles.

Albert Friedrich Huber
Albert Friedrich Huber was a Swiss banker who converted to Islam, financed Islamist organizations, and whose bank, Al-Taqwa, was accused of funding Osama bin Laden and al-Qaeda.

Gamal Abdel Nasser
Gamal Abdel Nasser was the Egyptian president who employed Johann von Leers after his migration to Argentina, providing him political cover.

Adolf Eichmann
Adolf Eichmann was a high-ranking Nazi official responsible for organizing the Holocaust; he was protected and aided by Swiss financiers including François Genoud and Albert Friedrich Huber.

Al-Taqwa Bank
Al-Taqwa Bank was a Swiss financial institution accused of financing Osama bin Laden and al-Qaeda, and was linked to Albert Friedrich Huber.

Adolf Hitler
Adolf Hitler was the leader of Nazi Germany; Johann von Leers was one of his most radical antisemitic propagandists during the Third Reich.

Christian Identity
Christian Identity is a racist and antisemitic interpretation of Christianity that influenced American and European far-right networks in the 1980s and served as a precursor to white jihadist ideology.

The Order
The Order is a 2023 film starring Jude Law that depicts the American white supremacist extremist milieu linked to Christian Identity and precursor movements to white jihad.

Aryan Nations
Aryan Nations is a white supremacist organization whose leaders actively sought alliances with al-Qaeda and promoted the ideology of David Myatt.

David Myatt
David Myatt is a British neo-Nazi and Islamist ideologue who authored texts justifying terrorist martyrdom operations and bridging neo-Nazi and jihadist ideologies.

t.me/c/3299898370/23
t.me/c/3299898370/23 is the direct Telegram link to the original post dated 2026-04-14, serving as the primary source of the text.

Ministry of Islamic Relations
Ministry of Islamic Relations was a communication channel used by Aryan Nations to establish contact with Middle Eastern jihadist groups.

Switzerland
Switzerland is the home country of François Genoud and Albert Friedrich Huber, whose financial networks supported Nazi war criminals and Islamist extremist groups.

Europe
Europe is a region where the white jihad ideology, initially developed in the U.S., is being gradually adopted and promoted by ultraright movements.

2026-04-14 15:58
2026-04-14 15:58 is the precise timestamp of the original post, marking the publication date and time of the documented ideological analysis.

Argentina
Argentina is the country where Johann von Leers (Omar Amin) fled after World War II and continued his ideological activities under the protection of Gamal Abdel Nasser’s network.

data.entity_name: A Practical Guide to Aryan Revolution
data.content:
A Practical Guide to Aryan Revolution
A Practical Guide to Aryan Revolution is a written work by David Myatt that provides ideological instruction for neo-Nazi revolutionaries and is cited as part of the fusion with jihadist thought.

data.entity_name: Are Martyrdom Operations Lawful According to the Quran and Sunnah?
data.content:
Are Martyrdom Operations Lawful According to the Quran and Sunnah?
Are Martyrdom Operations Lawful According to the Qur’an and Sunnah? is a treatise by David Myatt that attempts to legitimize suicide attacks through Islamic jurisprudence, bridging neo-Nazi and jihadist ideologies.

Радев
Радев is a politician who holds a majority and does not require partners to form a coalition.

Кремль
Кремль is an organization that supports any political forces, regardless of ideology, that aim to destabilize Europe from within.

Ультраправые антиукраинские силы
Ультраправые антиукраинские силы are ultraright political groups with anti-Ukrainian positions, allied with Фицо in a coalition.

Фицо
Фицо is a left-wing socialist politician who lacks a majority and formed a coalition with ultraright anti-Ukrainian forces.

Европа
Европа is the geographical and political region targeted for internal destabilization by forces aligned with Кремль.

Гнєсінська музична академія
Гнєсінська музична академія is a music institution in Moscow where Маттіас Моосдорф holds an honorary professorship.

Рюдігер Лукассен
Рюдігер Лукассен is a former defense spokesperson for the party who resigned after a conflict with Björn Höcke.

Вайдель
Вайдель is a political figure closely associated with Маркус Фронмаєр, influencing his recent political alignments.

Маркус Фронмаєр
Маркус Фронмаєр is the current foreign policy spokesperson for a political faction, previously linked to Russian structures and described in media as a "fully controlled deputy," and has recently cultivated ties with U.S. Republicans.

Маттіас Моосдорф
Маттіас Моосдорф is a former foreign policy spokesperson for a political faction, known for a consistent pro-Russian stance and holds an honorary professorship at the Gnessin Russian Academy of Music in Moscow.

Бйорн Хьоке
Бйорн Хьоке is a confirmed pro-Russian figure within the party and was involved in a conflict with Рюдігер Лукассен.

Конфлікт між Моосдорфом і Фронмаєром
The conflict between Моосдорф and Фронмаєр is a personal and ideological struggle over the party's foreign policy direction, with Фронмаєр attempting to displace Моосдорф as a rival.

Республіканці США
Республіканці США are a U.S. political party with which Маркус Фронмаєр has recently established contacts.

Конфлікт між Лукассеном і Хьоке
The conflict between Рюдігер Лукассен and Бйорн Хьоке led to the resignation of Рюдігер Лукассен from his position as defense spokesperson.

Cuba
Cuba is the target of U.S. tariffs due to its receipt of oil and petroleum products from other nations, deemed a threat to U.S. national security.

Ivan Dyakonov
Ivan Dyakonov is the author of the article reporting on Donald Trump’s tariff order against countries supplying oil to Cuba.

data.entity_name: Tariff Implementation Against Oil Suppliers to Cuba
data.content:
Tariff Implementation Against Oil Suppliers to Cuba
The Tariff Implementation Against Oil Suppliers to Cuba is a U.S. policy action imposing economic consequences on foreign nations that export oil to Cuba.

White House
The White House is the official source cited for the executive order regarding tariffs on oil suppliers to Cuba.

Гавана
Гавана is the capital city of Куба where the secret negotiations between США and Куба took place.

Рауль Гільєрмо Родрігес Кастро
Рауль Гільєрмо Родрігес Кастро is a Cuban official and grandson of former leader Raúl Castro, who met with the U.S. delegation during negotiations.

1959 революція
The 1959 revolution is the historical event during which assets of American citizens and corporations were confiscated by the Cuban government.

Тарас Григорович
Тарас Григорович is a source who reported on the secret negotiations between США and Куба via a Telegram channel.

Держдепартамент США
Держдепартамент США is the U.S. Department of State that led the delegation to Havana and presented demands regarding economic reforms, political prisoners, and compensation for seized assets.

Торгове ембарго
Торгове ембарго is the U.S. trade embargo against Куба, which may be lifted in exchange for reforms and concessions.

2016 візит Барака Обами
The 2016 visit by President Barack Obama to Куба was the last direct high-level engagement between the two countries before the 2026 negotiations.

Axios
Axios is a news source cited for reporting the arrival of the U.S. State Department delegation to Havana for negotiations with Cuba.

Starlink
Starlink is a satellite communication system offered by the United States as an incentive for Куба to comply with negotiated demands.

Німеччина
Німеччина is a European country whose far-right parties are critical of Donald Trump to avoid association with his toxic politics.

Ilulissat
Ilulissat is a coastal town in Greenland, depicted in the photograph, overlooking Disko Bay.

Ніколас Мадуро
Ніколас Мадуро is the leader of Venezuela who was removed from power by military force allegedly applied by Donald Trump.

Франція
Франція is a European country whose far-right parties are critical of Donald Trump to avoid association with his aggressive policies.

Європейські ультраправі партії
Європейські ультраправі партії are far-right political parties in Europe that are reassessing their ties to Donald Trump due to his aggressive policies.

Словаччина
Словаччина is a European country whose far-right parties, close to Russia, are forced to criticize Donald Trump.

Гренландія
Гренландія is a territory targeted by Donald Trump for potential annexation, sparking political controversy in Europe.

Disko Bay
Disko Bay is a body of water in Greenland, visible in the photograph with icebergs, used to illustrate the geopolitical context of Trump’s interest in Greenland.

Bloomberg
Bloomberg is the news organization that published the article and provided the photograph by Juliette Pavy, analyzing Trump’s Greenland policy.

Місце зйомки в Іллулісаті
The location of the photograph in Ilulissat, Greenland, captures the coastal infrastructure and Arctic environment relevant to the geopolitical narrative.

data.entity_name: Trumps Greenland Play Is a Step Too Far for Europes Far Right
data.content:
Trumps Greenland Play Is a Step Too Far for Europes Far Right
Trump’s Greenland Play Is a Step Too Far for Europe’s Far Right is an article by Bloomberg discussing the political backlash in Europe to Trump’s interest in annexing Greenland.

Juliette Pavy
Juliette Pavy is the photographer who captured the image of Ilulissat and Disko Bay for Bloomberg.

BILD
BILD is a German media outlet that commissioned Insa to conduct a survey on public opinion regarding support for Ukraine.

SPD
SPD (Social Democratic Party of Germany) is a political party in Germany whose voters show the highest level of solidarity with Ukraine at 74%.

Insa
Insa is an organization that conducted a public opinion survey on German support for Ukraine, commissioned by BILD.

BSW
BSW (Bündnis Sahra Wagenknecht) is a political party in Germany whose voters, like those of AfD, strongly oppose aid to Ukraine, with 65% rejecting it.

Greens
Greens is a political party in Germany whose voters exhibit high concern about potential Russian aggression in Eastern Europe, with 67% expressing anxiety.

NATO
NATO is the military alliance referenced as a potential target of Russian aggression following Ukraine, with Poland and Lithuania cited as examples.

Poland
Poland is a NATO member state cited as a potential target of Russian aggression after Ukraine.

Lithuania
Lithuania is a NATO member state cited as a potential target of Russian aggression after Ukraine.

Bundeswehr
The Bundeswehr is the German armed forces, identified as the operator of the PzH 2000 howitzer in the image.

Eastern Europe
Eastern Europe is the region where the PzH 2000 is likely deployed, based on terrain and geopolitical context.

PzH 2000
The PzH 2000 is a German self-propelled howitzer deployed in Eastern Europe, indicating active military operations.

Military Deployment
Military deployment refers to the observed positioning of the PzH 2000 in a forested area, suggesting training or operational activity related to regional security.

Russian Aggression
Russian aggression refers to the feared expansion of Russian military action beyond Ukraine into NATO territories.

Boer
"Boer" refers historically to descendants of Afrikaans-speaking colonists in South Africa, primarily white farmers, and is used in the chant as a symbol of colonial oppression.

Kill the Boer
"Kill the Boer" is a controversial apartheid-era chant, re-entoned by Julius Malema, which is interpreted by critics as inciting racial violence against white farmers in modern South Africa.

EFF
EFF is a radical left-wing political party in South Africa, led by Julius Malema, known for promoting contentious political rhetoric.

Derecha Libertaria
Derecha Libertaria is an Instagram account that shared content related to Julius Malema’s chant, framing it within a libertarian and right-wing political context.

Julius Malema
Julius Malema is the leader of the South African radical left-wing political party EFF and is seen chanting the controversial apartheid-era song "Kill the Boer" in front of a crowd of supporters.

South Africa
South Africa is the country where Julius Malema and the EFF operate, and where the historical and contemporary tensions around the term "Boer" and farmer violence are relevant.

Javier Milei
Javier Milei is referenced in the post’s hashtags as a figure associated with libertarian ideology, implicitly linking the critique of Malema’s chant to right-wing political discourse.

data.entity_name: Ultra Left and Ultra Right Channel
data.content:
Ultra Left and Ultra Right Channel
The Ultra Left and Ultra Right Channel is the Telegram channel that originally published the post on March 1, 2026, analyzing the ideological implications of Julius Malema’s chant.

data.entity_name: Telegram Channel "Ультра левые и ультра правые"
data.content:
Telegram Channel "Ультра левые и ультра правые"
The Telegram channel "Ультра левые и ультра правые" published the original post on March 1, 2026, analyzing the ideological implications of Julius Malema’s chant.

Instagram Reel DT_BzWeD9SF
